Convert a cumulative lifetime PD to an equivalent annual PD

Source: R/pd_horizon.R
pd_lifetime_to_annual.Rd

Under a constant-hazard (flat marginal) assumption, a cumulative probability of default `pd` over `term` years implies a per-year PD of \(1 - (1 - pd)^{1/term}\). Use this to place a TRISK term-structured PD into a 12-month IFRS-9 Stage 1 slot.

Usage

pd_lifetime_to_annual(pd, term)

Arguments

pd

Numeric vector of cumulative lifetime PDs in `[0, 1]`.

term

Numeric vector (recycled) of horizons in years, `> 0`.

Value

Numeric vector of equivalent annual PDs in `[0, 1]`. `NA` inputs propagate; `term <= 0` yields `NA` with a warning.

Choosing a direction (what to do based on your internal PD)

TRISK's `pd_baseline` / `pd_shock` are **cumulative over the loan `term`**. The package cannot know the horizon of a user-supplied `internal_pd`, so you must reconcile horizons yourself before [integrate_pd()] / [integrate_el()]:

  • **Internal PD is a 12-month (IFRS-9 Stage 1) PD** and you want to integrate it with TRISK's term PD: lift it to the term horizon first with `pd_annual_to_lifetime(internal_pd, term)`.

  • **Internal PD is already a lifetime/term PD** on the same horizon as the TRISK `term`: no conversion needed.

  • **You need a 12-month figure out of TRISK's term PD** (Stage 1 reporting): use `pd_lifetime_to_annual(pd, term)`.

Both directions assume a constant hazard, so the converted figure is an approximation (TRISK's curve is not flat).

See also

[pd_annual_to_lifetime()]

Examples

pd_lifetime_to_annual(0.10, term = 5)   # ~0.0209
#> [1] 0.02085164
